questions.text: Insert 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 into an empty BST, in that increasing order. Before you trace it: what will the resulting tree look like?
choices: Bushy and roughly balanced, similar in shape to the trees from earlier lessons
A single lopsided chain, each node with only a right child
It depends on the computer, there is no fixed shape
questions.explanation: Hold that thought. Trace the insertions below (each new value is always bigger than everything already there, so it always goes right) and you will see exactly why.
